zh.Staplehurst, Maidstone district, Kent, E: 英國英格蘭肯特斯塔普勒爾斯特
zh.Chilham, Kent, England, United Kingdom: 英國英格蘭肯特爾哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Ward於1540年出生在英國肯特爾哈姆時，他的父親Paul Ward21歲，母親Rio Chillham32歲。他於1574年10月25日與Parnell Bowles在英國肯特爾哈姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1585年之後在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Ward于1540年出生在英国肯特爾哈姆时，他的父亲Paul Ward21岁，母亲Rio Chillham32岁。他于1574年10月25日与Parnell Bowles在英国肯特爾哈姆结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1585年之后在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Ward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a watchman or guard, from Middle English ward ‘watchman, guard’ (Old English weard, used as both an agent noun and an abstract noun).
English: occupational name from Middle English warde ‘armed guard’ (Old English weard ‘watching, guarding’), with the same meaning as 1 above.

Irish: shortened form of McWard, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Bhaird ‘son of the poet’. The surname occurs throughout Ireland, where three different branches of the family are known as professional poets.

variants.name: Warshawsky
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Meyer, Ari, Hyman.

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from the city of Warsaw (Polish Warszawa), which became the capital of Poland at the end of the 16th century, after the destruction of Kraków by fire.
Americanized form of Polish Warszawski, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Wardman
meanings: English (Yorkshire): occupational name from Middle English wardman, either in the general sense ‘watchman, guard’ (compare Ward ) or in the administrative sense ‘representative of a ward within a borough’.

variants.name: Warde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Cormac, Donal.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Ward was born in 1540, in Chilham, Kent, England, his father, Paul Ward, was 21 and his mother, Rio Chillham, was 32. He married Parnell Bowles on 25 October 1574, in Chilham, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died after 1585, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Ward est né en 1540, à Chilham, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Paul Ward, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Rio Chillham, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Parnell Bowles le 25 octobre 1574, à Chilham, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé après 1585, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Ward 1540 in Chilham, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Paul Ward 21 und seine Mutter Rio Chillham 32. Er hat am 25. Oktober 1574 in Chilham, Kent, England Parnell Bowles geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb nach 1585 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Ward è nato il nel 1540 nel Chilham, Kent, England, il suo padre, Paul Ward, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Rio Chillham, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Parnell Bowles il 25 ottobre 1574, nel Chilham, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì dopo il 1585, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Wardが1540年にイギリスケントチルハムで生まれた時，父Paul Wardは21歳，母Rio Chillhamは32歳であった。1574年10月25日にイギリスケントチルハムでParnell Bowlesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1585年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Ward(이)가 1540년 영국 켄트 치함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Paul Ward(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Rio Chillham(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1574년 10월 25일 영국 켄트 치함에서 Parnell Bowles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1585년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Ward nasceu em 1540, em Chilham, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Paul Ward, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Rio Chillham, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Parnell Bowles em 25 de outubro de 1574, em Chilham, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu após 1585, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Ward родился в 1540 г. в местности чилхам, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Paul Ward было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Rio Chillham, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Parnell Bowles 25 октября 1574 г. в местности чилхам, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер после 1585 г. в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Ward nació en 1540, en Chilham, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Paul Ward, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Rio Chillham, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Parnell Bowles el 25 de octubre de 1574, en Chilham, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió después de 1585, en su pueblo.
